_**Agent (default)**_


Here is what was going wrong and what we changed.

### Cause
In Babel, a plugin name like `'react-native-dotenv'` is turned into the package **`babel-plugin-react-native-dotenv`**. That package is **not** in your project. The code you actually have installed is **`react-native-dotenv`**, which ships the plugin as its main entry.

### Fix
`babel.config.js` was updated to load the plugin from that package with the `module:` prefix and the usual options:

```19:26:c:\Users\Rishabh Gupta\Desktop\OTTReact\StreamList\Task2\StreamList\babel.config.js
    ['module:react-native-dotenv', {
      moduleName: '@env',
      path: '.env',
      safe: false,
      allowUndefined: true,
    }],
```

A local `react-native bundle` run completed successfully after this change (exit code 0).

### What you should do on the device
1. Stop Metro if it is running.
2. Start again with a clean cache:  
   `npx react-native start --port 8082 --reset-cache`
3. Reload the app on the phone (shake menu → Reload, or run `adb shell input keyevent 82` if you use that flow).

No new dependency was added; `react-native-dotenv` was already in `package.json`.

**File updated:** `Task2/StreamList/babel.config.js`

Here is the updated `tsconfig.json`:

```json
{
  "extends": "@react-native/typescript-config",
  "compilerOptions": {
    "types": ["jest"],
    "strict": true,
    "noImplicitAny": true,
    "strictNullChecks": true
  },
  "include": ["**/*.ts", "**/*.tsx"],
  "exclude": ["**/node_modules", "**/Pods"]
}
```

**Note:** With `"strict": true`, `noImplicitAny` and `strictNullChecks` are already turned on. Listing them again only makes that explicit and does not change behavior.

### 1. `.env` (project root: `Task2/StreamList/.env`)

Created with your three variables. The real token is stored in the file on disk; it is **not** repeated here.

export const colors = {
  // Surfaces — layered from deepest to brightest
  surface:                   '#131313',  // Main app background
  surface_container_lowest:  '#0E0E0E',  // Deepest recessed elements
  surface_container_low:     '#1C1B1B',  // Section groupings
  surface_container:         '#232323',  // Mid-level containers
  surface_container_high:    '#2A2A2A',  // Elevated sections
  surface_container_highest: '#353534',  // Cards, interactive elements
  surface_bright:            '#3A3939',  // Hover/pressed states

  // Primary accent (Coral-Red)
  primary:                   '#FFB3AE',  // Gradient start / light tint
  primary_container:         '#FF5351',  // Gradient end / strong accent
  secondary_container:       '#822625',  // Active chip state (deep red)

  // Text
  on_surface:                '#E5E2E1',  // Primary text — never use pure white

  // Surfaces — layered from deepest to brightest
  surface:                   '#131313',  // Main app background
  surface_container_lowest:  '#0E0E0E',  // Deepest recessed elements
  surface_container_low:     '#1C1B1B',  // Section groupings
  surface_container:         '#232323',  // Mid-level containers
  surface_container_high:    '#2A2A2A',  // Elevated sections
  surface_container_highest: '#353534',  // Cards, interactive elements
  surface_bright:            '#3A3939',  // Hover/pressed states

  // Primary accent (Coral-Red)
  primary:                   '#FFB3AE',  // Gradient start / light tint
  primary_container:         '#FF5351',  // Gradient end / strong accent
  secondary_container:       '#822625',  // Active chip state (deep red)

  // Text

};

Summary of what was added to `src/api/types.ts`:

### Nullability
Fields TMDB often sends as JSON `null` use unions with `null`:
- **`Movie` / `MovieDetail`:** `poster_path`, `backdrop_path`
- **`MovieDetail`:** `runtime` (unknown / not applicable)
- **`Cast`:** `profile_path`

### Non-null fields
- **`release_date`**, **`overview`**, **`title`**, **`vote_average`**, **`genre_ids`**, **`genres`**, **`name`**, **`character`**, etc. are required and non-null. TMDB still uses empty strings where there is no text; those stay as `string`, not `string | null`.
- No `any`, no unnecessary `?`.

### Interfaces
| Interface | Role |
|-----------|------|
| `Genre` | `id`, `name` |
| `Movie` | List-style movie row with `genre_ids` |
| `MovieDetail` | Detail row with `genres: Genre[]` and `runtime` |
| `Cast` | Cast line item |
| `Credits` | `id` + `cast` (matches the slice of `/credits` you care about) |
| `PaginatedResponse<T>` | `page`, `results`, `total_pages`, `total_results` |

Full file:

```1:52:c:\Users\Rishabh Gupta\Desktop\OTTReact\StreamList\Task2\StreamList\src\api\types.ts
export interface Genre {
  id: number;
  name: string;
}

export interface Movie {
  id: number;
  title: string;
  poster_path: string | null;
  backdrop_path: string | null;
  vote_average: number;
  release_date: string;
  genre_ids: number[];
  overview: string;
}

export interface MovieDetail {
  id: number;
  title: string;
  poster_path: string | null;
  backdrop_path: string | null;
  vote_average: number;
  release_date: string;
  genres: Genre[];
  runtime: number | null;
  overview: string;
}

export interface Cast {
  id: number;
  name: string;
  character: string;
  profile_path: string | null;
}

export interface Credits {
  id: number;
  cast: Cast[];
}

export interface PaginatedResponse<T> {
  page: number;
  results: T[];
  total_pages: number;
  total_results: number;
}
```

_**Agent (default)**_


Here’s what `src/api/client.ts` does:

### Behaviour
1. **Env** — `baseURL` from `TMDB_BASE_URL` and the bearer token from `TMDB_ACCESS_TOKEN` via `@env` (no literals).
2. **Request interceptor** — Sets `Authorization: Bearer ${TMDB_ACCESS_TOKEN}` on every request.
3. **Response error path** — Rejects with `{ message: string; status: number }`: HTTP status from `response.status` when present, otherwise `0`; message from TMDB `status_message` / generic `message` in JSON body, else `error.message`, else `'Request failed'`.
4. **Retry** — If there is **no** `response` (timeouts `ECONNABORTED` / `ETIMEDOUT`, `ERR_NETWORK`, or request sent with no response), the same config is retried **once** via `__retryCount`. Any **4xx/5xx** (or anything with `error.response`) is **not** retried.
5. **Public API** — **Default export only** — the configured Axios instance.

### Implementation notes
- `movies.ts` (and the rest of the app) should use `import client from '@api/client'` or `from '../api/client'` and **must not** import `axios` anywhere else.
- Failed requests reject with a **plain object**, not `AxiosError`, so hooks should treat errors as `{ message, status }`.
- `timeout: 30_000` is set so timeouts are distinguishable from hanging forever.

`npx tsc --noEmit` completes successfully for this project.

### Details
- **`TmdbImageSize`** — `'w185' | 'w342' | 'w780' | 'original'`.
- **`buildTmdbImageUrl(path, size)`** — Uses `TMDB_IMAGE_BASE_URL` from `@env`. Returns `null` when `path` is `null` or `''` (empty string would not be a valid TMDB file path and would avoid a useless URL). Otherwise returns `` `${TMDB_IMAGE_BASE_URL}/${size}${path}` `` (TMDB paths already include a leading `/`).

```1:16:c:\Users\Rishabh Gupta\Desktop\OTTReact\StreamList\Task2\StreamList\src\utils\image.ts
import { TMDB_IMAGE_BASE_URL } from '@env';

export type TmdbImageSize = 'w185' | 'w342' | 'w780' | 'original';

export function buildTmdbImageUrl(
  path: string | null,
  size: TmdbImageSize,
): string | null {
  if (path === null || path.length === 0) {
    return null;
  }
  return `${TMDB_IMAGE_BASE_URL}/${size}${path}`;
}
```

If you want **`null` only when `path === null`** and to allow empty strings (not recommended), say so and we can drop the `path.length === 0` check.

Populate `src/store/watchlistStore.ts` using Zustand. Implement the following exactly:

Interfaces:
interface WatchlistItem {
  id: number;
  title: string;
  posterPath: string | null;
  voteAverage: number;
  releaseDate: string;
  genreIds: number[];
  mediaType: 'movie' | 'tv';
}

interface WatchlistStore {
  items: WatchlistItem[];
  addItem: (item: WatchlistItem) => void;
  removeItem: (id: number) => void;
  isInWatchlist: (id: number) => boolean;
  count: number;
  hydrated: boolean;
}

Requirements:
1. Use Zustand's `persist` middleware with `@react-native-async-storage/async-storage` as the storage adapter.
2. `addItem` must check for duplicates — if an item with the same id already exists, do nothing.
3. `removeItem` removes by id.
4. `count` must be a derived value equal to `items.length` — not a separate state field that could go out of sync.
5. `hydrated` must be set to true only after AsyncStorage has loaded. Use Zustand's `onRehydrateStorage` callback to set it.
6. No watchlist UI should render until `hydrated` is true — this prevents stale button states on app start.

Here’s what was added to `src/store/watchlistStore.ts`.

### Behaviour
- **`persist`** with `createJSONStorage(() => AsyncStorage)` and **`partialize`** so only **`items`** are stored (not `hydrated` or actions).
- **`addItem`** — appends only if no existing item has the same **`id`**; otherwise leaves state unchanged.
- **`removeItem`** — drops items whose **`id`** matches.
- **`isInWatchlist`** — `items.some(i => i.id === id)`.
- **`count`** — accessor backed by **`get().items.length`** (not persisted, not a separate counter).
- **`hydrated`** — starts **`false`**; set to **`true`** in **`onRehydrateStorage`**’s post-rehydration callback (runs after load, including failures / empty storage).

### UI note
Gate watchlist UI on **`useWatchlistStore((s) => s.hydrated)`** (or equivalent) so buttons/lists don’t flash wrong state before AsyncStorage finishes.

### TMDB caveat
Duplicate detection is **by `id` only**. TMDB can reuse the same numeric id for a movie and a TV show; if you need uniqueness per medium, extend the key later (e.g. composite with `mediaType`).

```1:58:c:\Users\Rishabh Gupta\Desktop\OTTReact\StreamList\Task2\StreamList\src\store\watchlistStore.ts
import AsyncStorage from '@react-native-async-storage/async-storage';
import { create } from 'zustand';
import { createJSONStorage, persist } from 'zustand/middleware';

export interface WatchlistItem {
  id: number;
  title: string;
  posterPath: string | null;
  voteAverage: number;
  releaseDate: string;
  genreIds: number[];
  mediaType: 'movie' | 'tv';
}

export interface WatchlistStore {
  items: WatchlistItem[];
  addItem: (item: WatchlistItem) => void;
  removeItem: (id: number) => void;
  isInWatchlist: (id: number) => boolean;
  count: number;
  hydrated: boolean;
}

export const useWatchlistStore = create<WatchlistStore>()(
  persist(
    (set, get) => ({
      items: [],
      hydrated: false,
      addItem: (item) => {
        set((state) => {
          if (state.items.some((i) => i.id === item.id)) {
            return state;
          }
          return { items: [...state.items, item] };
        });
      },
      removeItem: (id) => {
        set((state) => ({
          items: state.items.filter((i) => i.id !== id),
        }));
      },
      isInWatchlist: (id) => get().items.some((i) => i.id === id),
      get count() {
        return get().items.length;
      },
    }),
    {
      name: 'watchlist-storage',
      storage: createJSONStorage(() => AsyncStorage),
      partialize: (state) => ({ items: state.items }),
      onRehydrateStorage: () => () => {
        useWatchlistStore.setState({ hydrated: true });
      },
    },
  ),
);
```

`npx tsc --noEmit` passes for the project.

Here’s what was added to `src/navigation/types.ts`.

### Contents
1. **`RootTabParamList`** — `Home`, `Search`, `Watchlist`, and `Profile` are all `undefined`.
2. **Stacks** — `HomeStackParamList`, `SearchStackParamList`, and `WatchlistStackParamList` each have a main screen (`*Main`: `undefined`) and **`Detail: { movieId: number }`**. Profile is only a tab (no stack list), as in your spec.
3. **Helpers** — For each stack, **`NativeStackNavigationProp`**-based navigation types and **`RouteProp`** route types (from `@react-navigation/native`).

### Typed usage
```typescript
import { useNavigation, useRoute } from '@react-navigation/native';
import type {
  HomeStackNavigationProp,
  HomeStackRouteProp,
} from '@navigation/types';

// In a Home stack screen:
const navigation = useNavigation<HomeStackNavigationProp>();
navigation.navigate('Detail', { movieId: 550 });

const route = useRoute<HomeStackRouteProp<'Detail'>>();
// route.params.movieId
```

Use the matching `*StackNavigationProp` / `*StackRouteProp` for Search and Watchlist stacks.

```1:52:c:\Users\Rishabh Gupta\Desktop\OTTReact\StreamList\Task2\StreamList\src\navigation\types.ts
import type { RouteProp } from '@react-navigation/native';
import type { NativeStackNavigationProp } from '@react-navigation/native-stack';

export type RootTabParamList = {
  Home: undefined;
  Search: undefined;
  Watchlist: undefined;
  Profile: undefined;
};

export type HomeStackParamList = {
  HomeMain: undefined;
  Detail: { movieId: number };
};

export type SearchStackParamList = {
  SearchMain: undefined;
  Detail: { movieId: number };
};

export type WatchlistStackParamList = {
  WatchlistMain: undefined;
  Detail: { movieId: number };
};

export type HomeStackNavigationProp<
  T extends keyof HomeStackParamList = keyof HomeStackParamList,
> = NativeStackNavigationProp<HomeStackParamList, T>;

export type HomeStackRouteProp<T extends keyof HomeStackParamList> = RouteProp<
  HomeStackParamList,
  T
>;

export type SearchStackNavigationProp<
  T extends keyof SearchStackParamList = keyof SearchStackParamList,
> = NativeStackNavigationProp<SearchStackParamList, T>;

export type SearchStackRouteProp<T extends keyof SearchStackParamList> =
  RouteProp<SearchStackParamList, T>;

export type WatchlistStackNavigationProp<
  T extends keyof WatchlistStackParamList = keyof WatchlistStackParamList,
> = NativeStackNavigationProp<WatchlistStackParamList, T>;

export type WatchlistStackRouteProp<T extends keyof WatchlistStackParamList> =
  RouteProp<WatchlistStackParamList, T>;
```

When you wire nested navigators, you can later extend the root list with `NavigatorScreenParams<HomeStackParamList>` for `Home`, etc., if you want tab-level typing for nested routes; your current requirement was tabs with no params, so it stays as above.

Populate `src/navigation/RootNavigator.tsx` with a bottom tab navigator. Requirements:

1. Four tabs: Home, Search, Watchlist, Profile.
2. Each tab (except Profile) wraps a native stack navigator so that navigating to Detail from Search returns to Search on back — not Home.
3. Detail screen is registered inside each stack's navigator, not at the tab level.
4. Tab bar styling:
   - Background: `rgba(35, 35, 35, 0.70)` using BlurView from `@react-native-community/blur` with `blurAmount: 20`.
   - Active icon color: `#FF5351` (from colors.primary_container).
   - Inactive icon color: `#E4BDBA` (from colors.on_surface_variant).
   - No label text under icons.
   - Tab bar must float above content (absolute position at bottom).
5. Watchlist tab shows a numeric badge using the `count` value from the Zustand watchlist store. Badge is only visible when count > 0. Badge color: `#FF5351`.
6. Profile tab does nothing when tapped (placeholder).
7. Use placeholder screen components for now — just a View with a Text saying the screen name.



---

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
**Updating navigation setup**

I'm updating the navigation setup to support nested stacks and tabs with proper typing. I'm implementing the tab bar with BlurView background and proper color overrides. </details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_


Here’s what was implemented.

### `src/navigation/RootNavigator.tsx`
- **Tabs:** `Home`, `Search`, `Watchlist`, `Profile`.
- **Stacks:** `HomeStackNavigator`, `SearchStackNavigator`, and `WatchlistStackNavigator` each use `createNativeStackNavigator` with `*Main` + **`Detail`** (with `movieId` from `route.params`). **`Profile`** is a single tab screen (`ProfileTabScreen`) — placeholder only.
- **Tab bar:** `position: 'absolute'`, `left` / `right` / `bottom` from `spacing.sm`, height from theme spacing + `useSafeAreaInsets().bottom`, `tabBarBackground` = `BlurView` (`blurType="dark"`, `blurAmount={20}`) plus a full-screen tint using **`colors.tab_bar_overlay`** (`rgba(35, 35, 35, 0.70)` in `colors.ts`).
- **Colors:** Active = `colors.primary_container`, inactive = `colors.on_surface_variant` (same as `#FF5351` / `#E4BDBA`).
- **Labels:** `tabBarShowLabel: false`.
- **Watchlist badge:** `tabBarBadge` when `hydrated && count > 0`; `tabBarBadgeStyle.backgroundColor = colors.primary_container`.
- **Placeholders:** Centered `View` + `Text` with the screen name; `Detail` shows `Detail` and `movieId`.
- **Icons:** Unicode symbols in `Text` (no extra icon dependency). Swap for real icons later if you want.

Imports use **relative** paths (`../theme/...`, `../store/...`) so `tsc` resolves them without extra `paths` in `tsconfig`.

### `src/navigation/types.ts`
- **`RootTabParamList`** now uses **`NavigatorScreenParams<...StackParamList>`** for `Home`, `Search`, and `Watchlist` so nested stacks stay type-safe; **`Profile`** stays `undefined`.

### `src/theme/colors.ts`
- **`tab_bar_overlay: 'rgba(35, 35, 35, 0.70)'`** so the tab bar tint isn’t hardcoded in the navigator (per your theme rules).

### `App.tsx`
- Wraps the app with **`NavigationContainer`** and **`RootNavigator`** (still inside **`SafeAreaProvider`**).

### Jest
- **`jest.config.js`:** `transformIgnorePatterns` for `@react-navigation`, plus **`setupFiles: ['<rootDir>/jest.setup.js']`**.
- **`jest.setup.js`:** Mocks **AsyncStorage** (official mock) and **`BlurView`** (as `View`) so `App.test.tsx` passes.

`npx tsc --noEmit` and **`npm test`** both succeed.

**Files touched:** `src/navigation/RootNavigator.tsx`, `src/navigation/types.ts`, `src/theme/colors.ts`, `App.tsx`, `jest.config.js`, `jest.setup.js` (new).

---

_**User**_

Update `App.tsx` to:
1. Wrap the app in `NavigationContainer` from `@react-navigation/native`.
2. Render `RootNavigator` inside it.
3. Wrap the root in `SafeAreaProvider` from `react-native-safe-area-context`.

Then run the app on Android emulator using:
npx react-native run-android --port=8082

Confirm the following before saying done:
- The bottom tab bar is visible with 4 tabs (Home, Search, Watchlist, Profile).
- Tapping each tab shows the placeholder screen with the screen name.
- The Watchlist tab shows a numeric badge when items exist.
- No TypeScript errors in the terminal.
- No red error screen on the emulator.

Note: This is a Windows machine using Android emulator only. Do NOT run any iOS or pod install commands.


---

### Command run
`npx react-native run-android --port=8082` completed with **`BUILD SUCCESSFUL in 1m 7s`**, APK installed, and the app **started on `emulator-5554`** (and another device). Metro reported **port 8082** and **“Connecting to the development server…”** with no Gradle/CLI failure.

### What I can confirm from the machine (terminal / build)
| Check | Result |
|--------|--------|
| **No TypeScript errors** | `npx tsc --noEmit` exited **0**. |
| **Android build** | **BUILD SUCCESSFUL**; `MainActivity` launched on the emulator. |
| **Red error screen on emulator** | **Not verifiable here** — there’s no screenshot/log of the JS runtime. If Metro serves the bundle without a 500, you’re usually fine; please glance at the emulator once. |

### What follows from your codebase (not from live UI inspection)
| Check | Basis |
|--------|--------|
| **Four tabs (Home, Search, Watchlist, Profile)** | `RootNavigator.tsx` defines those four `Tab.Screen`s. |
| **Placeholders with screen names** | Each stack uses `PlaceholderScreen` with `HomeMain`, `SearchMain`, `WatchlistMain`, and `Profile` uses `ProfileTabScreen` → `PlaceholderScreen title="Profile"`. |
| **Watchlist badge when items exist** | `tabBarBadge` is set when `watchlistHydrated && watchlistCount > 0` (Zustand). With an empty watchlist the badge stays hidden until you add items. |

**Done from this environment:** `App.tsx` layout is correct, TypeScript is clean, and **`run-android --port=8082` succeeded and launched the app on the Android emulator.**  
**Please confirm on the emulator:** tabs, placeholders, badge after adding to the watchlist, and that you don’t see the red RN error screen.

_**User**_

The app is running but needs these fixes before Day 1 is complete:

1. Remove the white header bar at the top of every screen. Set the navigation header to hidden or give it a dark background matching colors.surface (#131313).

2. The tab bar background should be dark with glassmorphism effect using rgba(35, 35, 35, 0.70). Make sure it is styled correctly.

3. Set the tab bar to show NO labels — icons only. Remove any tab label text.

4. Make sure the overall app background color is #131313 (dark) on all placeholder screens — not white or default gray.

After fixing, run again on Android emulator with:
npx react-native run-android --port=8082
